name: system-control
description: Open files, folders, programs, and manage terminal sessions (CMD, PowerShell). Close running processes. Full system control from chat.
author: AGNT User

schema.title: Open File
schema.description: Open a file with its default associated program (e.g. open a .docx in Word, .png in Photos, .pdf in browser)

filePath.label: File Path
filePath.description: Absolute or relative path to the file (e.g. C:\Users\jacks\report.docx)

selectInFolder.label: Select in Folder
selectInFolder.description: If checked, opens the containing folder and highlights the file instead of launching it

pid.description: Process ID of the launched application (if available)

schema.title: Open Folder
schema.description: Open a folder in Windows Explorer

folderPath.label: Folder Path
folderPath.description: Absolute or relative path to the folder (e.g. C:\Users\jacks\Documents)

schema.title: Open App / URL
schema.description: Open a URL in the default browser or launch a program by name/path (e.g. 'notepad', 'calc', 'https://google.com')

target.label: Target
target.description: URL (https://...), program name (notepad), or program path (C:\Windows\System32\mspaint.exe)

arguments.label: Arguments
arguments.description: Optional command-line arguments to pass to the program (space-separated)

workingDirectory.label: Working Directory
workingDirectory.description: Optional working directory for the launched process

pid.description: Process ID of the launched application

schema.title: Terminal Session
schema.description: Manage terminal sessions — open CMD or PowerShell, send commands, read output, and close sessions. Sessions are persisted so you can send multiple commands to the same shell.

action.label: Action
action.description: What to do with the terminal session

sessionId.label: Session ID
sessionId.description: Unique ID for the terminal session (required for exec/read/close). Use any name like 'my-cmd' or 'deploy-shell'.

shell.label: Shell Type
shell.description: Which shell to open (only used with 'open' action)

command.label: Command
command.description: Command(s) to execute in the session (required for 'exec' action). Supports multi-line.

workingDirectory.label: Working Directory
workingDirectory.description: Starting directory for new sessions (only used with 'open' action)

timeout.label: Timeout (ms)
timeout.description: How long to wait for command output in milliseconds (default: 5000)

sessionId.description: The session ID (useful when opening new sessions)

exitCode.description: Exit code of the last command (-1 if still running)

schema.title: Close Process
schema.description: Close/kill a running process by PID or process name. Can also kill all matching processes.

pid.label: Process ID (PID)
pid.description: Numeric PID of the process to kill. Either PID or Process Name is required.

processName.label: Process Name
processName.description: Name of the process to kill (e.g. 'notepad.exe', 'chrome.exe'). Either PID or Process Name is required.

killAll.label: Kill All Matching
killAll.description: If checked and using process name, kill ALL matching processes (e.g. all chrome.exe)

force.label: Force Kill
force.description: Force kill the process immediately (without graceful shutdown)

killed.description: List of killed processes with PID and name
